terms.definition: A scalar metric (0–1) expressing the weighted average alignment of a civilization’s critical subsystems.
The degree to which parts of a social system remain internally consistent, mutually reinforcing, and capable of coordinated action.
An abrupt shift in a system from one stable state to another when a control parameter crosses a critical threshold.
A configuration in which a system’s macro-level variables remain approximately constant absent external perturbation.
The model-specific value of χ below which systemic feedback loops drive rapid fragmentation.
Mutual causal linkage among distinct functional domains of a complex system.
A self-reinforcing sequence of failures propagating through a network once initiated.
Metaphoric label for a subsystem whose integrity is disproportionately essential to overall systemic stability.
The process by which cultural norms, social capital, and resources are passed from one generation to the next.
